beg.desc: Use let and const to declare variables. const for values that don't change, let for those that do.
beg.usecase: Real-world use: Storing user data and app state.

int.desc: Understand hoisting, temporal dead zone, and block scoping differences between var, let, and const.
int.usecase: Real-world use: Avoiding closure bugs in loops.

pro.desc: Use Object.freeze and const with destructured defaults for immutable configuration patterns.
pro.usecase: Real-world use: Immutable app configuration in production.

beg.desc: JavaScript has 7 primitive types: string, number, boolean, null, undefined, symbol, bigint.
beg.code:
let str = "hello";    // string
let num = 42;          // number
let bool = true;       // boolean
let nothing = null;    // null
let undef;             // undefined
console.log(typeof str, typeof num);
beg.usecase: Real-world use: Understanding data flowing through your application.

int.desc: Understand implicit coercion rules and use strict equality to avoid subtle bugs.
int.code:
console.log(1 + '2');      // '12' (string concat)
console.log('5' - 3);      // 2 (numeric)
console.log('' == false);   // true (coercion)
console.log('' === false);  // false (strict)
console.log(null == undefined); // true
console.log(null === undefined); // false
int.usecase: Real-world use: Preventing type coercion bugs in form validation.

pro.desc: Use Symbol for unique property keys and BigInt for arbitrary-precision integers.
pro.usecase: Real-world use: Unique object keys in libraries and large number math.

beg.desc: Strings hold text. Template literals use backticks for interpolation and multi-line strings.
beg.usecase: Real-world use: Dynamic message formatting.

int.desc: Use string methods like slice, includes, replace, and split for text processing.
int.usecase: Real-world use: Parsing CSV data and manipulating URLs.

pro.desc: Use tagged templates for safe HTML rendering and SQL query building.
pro.usecase: Real-world use: XSS-safe template rendering in web apps.

beg.desc: JavaScript has one number type (64-bit float). Use Math for common calculations.
beg.usecase: Real-world use: Price calculations and random number generation.

int.desc: Handle floating-point precision issues and use Number methods for parsing and validation.
int.code:
console.log(0.1 + 0.2 === 0.3); // false!
console.log(Math.abs(0.1 + 0.2 - 0.3) < Number.EPSILON); // true

console.log(Number.isInteger(5));     // true
console.log(Number.isFinite(1/0));    // false
console.log(Number.parseFloat('3.14px')); // 3.14
int.usecase: Real-world use: Reliable financial calculations.

pro.desc: Use Intl.NumberFormat for locale-aware number formatting across different currencies and regions.
pro.usecase: Real-world use: Multi-currency display in global e-commerce platforms.

beg.desc: Arrays are ordered lists. Use push, pop, and indexing to manage elements.
beg.usecase: Real-world use: Managing lists of items like shopping carts.

int.desc: Use map, filter, reduce, find, and every for functional array processing.
int.usecase: Real-world use: Processing API response data.

pro.desc: Use Array.from, flatMap, and custom iterators for advanced data transformation pipelines.
pro.usecase: Real-world use: Batch processing and data pipeline operations.

beg.desc: Objects store key-value pairs. Access properties with dot or bracket notation.
beg.usecase: Real-world use: Representing entities like users and products.

int.desc: Use computed properties, shorthand syntax, and Object methods for dynamic objects.
int.usecase: Real-world use: Dynamic form data construction and merging configs.

pro.desc: Use Object.defineProperty and Proxy for reactive objects with getter/setter traps.
pro.usecase: Real-world use: Reactive state management like Vue.js reactivity system.

beg.desc: Functions are reusable code blocks. They can take parameters and return values.
beg.usecase: Real-world use: Encapsulating reusable logic.

int.desc: Understand closures, IIFE patterns, and function scope for data privacy.
int.usecase: Real-world use: Private state encapsulation in modules.

pro.desc: Use higher-order functions and function composition for declarative code architecture.
pro.usecase: Real-world use: Data transformation pipelines in functional architecture.

beg.desc: Arrow functions are a shorter syntax for writing functions.
beg.usecase: Real-world use: Concise callbacks in array methods.

int.desc: Arrow functions don't have their own 'this' — they inherit it from the enclosing scope.
int.usecase: Real-world use: Event handlers inside classes without binding.

pro.desc: Use arrow functions for implicit returns in point-free style and curried function factories.
pro.usecase: Real-world use: Reusable formatting utilities with currying.

beg.desc: Use if/else and ternary operators to make decisions in your code.
beg.usecase: Real-world use: Access control based on user age.

int.desc: Use switch statements and optional chaining for multi-branch logic and null safety.
int.usecase: Real-world use: Safe property access in nested API responses.

pro.desc: Use nullish coalescing, logical assignment, and strategy pattern for clean branching.
pro.usecase: Real-world use: Pluggable serialization strategies in data export.

beg.desc: Loops repeat code. Use for, for...of, and while for different iteration needs.
beg.usecase: Real-world use: Iterating through lists of data.

int.desc: Use for...in for object keys, forEach for side effects, and break/continue for flow control.
int.usecase: Real-world use: Processing configuration objects and finding specific data.

pro.desc: Use custom iterables with Symbol.iterator for domain-specific iteration patterns.
pro.usecase: Real-world use: Custom pagination iterators for data streams.

beg.desc: Destructuring extracts values from arrays and objects into variables.
beg.usecase: Real-world use: Extracting data from API responses.

int.desc: Use nested destructuring, default values, and rest patterns for complex data extraction.
int.usecase: Real-world use: Extracting nested data from GraphQL responses.

pro.desc: Use destructuring in function parameters for clean API design and config objects.
pro.usecase: Real-world use: Express.js-style server configuration.

beg.desc: Spread (...) expands arrays/objects. Rest (...) collects remaining elements.
beg.usecase: Real-world use: Copying and extending arrays and objects.

int.desc: Use rest parameters in functions and spread for merging objects with overrides.
int.usecase: Real-world use: Merging default and user configurations.

pro.desc: Use spread with structuredClone for deep copies and immutable state updates.
pro.usecase: Real-world use: Immutable state management in React/Redux.

beg.desc: Promises handle asynchronous operations. async/await makes them look synchronous.
beg.usecase: Real-world use: Waiting for API responses or timers.

int.desc: Use Promise.all for parallel execution and try/catch for async error handling.
int.usecase: Real-world use: Loading multiple API endpoints in parallel.

pro.desc: Use Promise.allSettled, AbortController, and async generators for production async patterns.
pro.usecase: Real-world use: Timeout-protected API calls in production apps.

beg.desc: Use try/catch to handle errors gracefully without crashing your program.
beg.usecase: Real-world use: Handling invalid JSON from user input.

int.desc: Create custom error classes for domain-specific error handling.
int.usecase: Real-world use: Form validation with specific error types.

pro.desc: Use Error.cause chaining and global error boundaries for production error management.
pro.usecase: Real-world use: Error cause chains for debugging in microservices.

beg.desc: The DOM lets JavaScript interact with HTML elements on the page.
beg.usecase: Real-world use: Dynamically updating page content.

int.desc: Use event delegation, dataset attributes, and DocumentFragment for efficient DOM updates.
int.usecase: Real-world use: Efficient event handling for dynamic lists.

pro.desc: Use MutationObserver and requestAnimationFrame for performance-optimized DOM updates.
pro.usecase: Real-world use: High-performance rendering of large lists.

beg.desc: Events respond to user actions like clicks, key presses, and form submissions.
beg.usecase: Real-world use: Responding to user interactions.

int.desc: Use event bubbling, preventDefault, and custom events for complex interactions.
int.usecase: Real-world use: Form handling and custom event systems.

pro.desc: Use AbortController to manage event listener lifecycles and prevent memory leaks.
pro.usecase: Real-world use: Component lifecycle cleanup in SPA frameworks.

beg.desc: The Fetch API makes HTTP requests to servers and APIs.
beg.code:
fetch('https://jsonplaceholder.typicode.com/posts/1')
  .then(response => response.json())
  .then(data => console.log(data.title))
  .catch(error => console.error('Error:', error));
beg.usecase: Real-world use: Loading data from REST APIs.

int.desc: Use async/await with fetch for POST requests and custom headers.
int.usecase: Real-world use: Sending form data to backend APIs.

pro.desc: Build a fetch wrapper with interceptors, retry logic, and request/response transformations.
pro.usecase: Real-world use: Centralized API client with auth interceptors.

beg.desc: A closure is a function that remembers variables from where it was created.
beg.usecase: Real-world use: Creating specialized functions from templates.

int.desc: Use closures for data privacy, memoization, and factory functions.
int.usecase: Real-world use: Caching expensive computations.

pro.desc: Use closures with WeakRef for memory-efficient caching that doesn't prevent garbage collection.
pro.usecase: Real-world use: Memory-sensitive caching in long-running applications.

beg.desc: Classes are templates for creating objects with shared methods.
beg.usecase: Real-world use: Object-oriented programming with inheritance.

int.desc: Use getters, setters, static methods, and private fields for encapsulation.
int.usecase: Real-world use: Secure data encapsulation with private fields.

pro.desc: Use mixins and Symbol-based protocols for flexible composition over inheritance.
pro.usecase: Real-world use: Composable mixins for cross-cutting concerns.

beg.desc: Modules let you split code into separate files with import and export.
beg.usecase: Real-world use: Organizing code into reusable files.

int.desc: Use default exports, re-exports, and dynamic imports for flexible module architecture.
int.usecase: Real-world use: Code splitting and lazy loading for performance.

pro.desc: Use module patterns with barrel exports and import maps for large-scale project organization.
pro.usecase: Real-world use: Scalable module architecture in enterprise applications.

beg.desc: Map holds key-value pairs with any key type. Set holds unique values.
beg.usecase: Real-world use: Deduplication and flexible key-value storage.

int.desc: Use Map for frequency counting and Set for efficient membership testing.
int.usecase: Real-world use: Text analysis and search indexing.

pro.desc: Use WeakMap for metadata storage that doesn't prevent garbage collection.
pro.usecase: Real-world use: Private metadata storage in UI component frameworks.

beg.desc: Regular expressions match patterns in strings for search and validation.
beg.usecase: Real-world use: Email and phone number validation.

int.desc: Use capture groups, replace with callbacks, and named groups for structured extraction.
int.usecase: Real-world use: Template string interpolation and date parsing.

pro.desc: Use lookbehind, lookahead, and the 'd' flag for advanced pattern matching with indices.
pro.usecase: Real-world use: Building lexers and parsers for DSLs.

beg.desc: JSON is the standard format for sending data between servers and web apps.
beg.usecase: Real-world use: Storing and transmitting structured data.

int.desc: Use replacer and reviver functions for custom serialization logic.
int.usecase: Real-world use: Secure serialization excluding sensitive fields.

pro.desc: Use structuredClone and streaming JSON parsing for large datasets.
pro.usecase: Real-world use: Deep cloning complex state and streaming log parsing.

beg.desc: LocalStorage persists data in the browser even after closing the tab.
beg.usecase: Real-world use: Saving user preferences across sessions.

int.desc: Build a typed storage wrapper with expiration and event listeners.
int.usecase: Real-world use: Token storage with automatic expiration.

pro.desc: Use IndexedDB for structured, indexed storage of large datasets in the browser.
pro.usecase: Real-world use: Offline-first apps with client-side databases.

beg.desc: Design patterns are proven solutions to common programming problems.
beg.usecase: Real-world use: Single database connection instance.

int.desc: Use Observer pattern for event-driven architectures and loose coupling.
int.usecase: Real-world use: Pub/sub messaging in reactive applications.

pro.desc: Use Builder pattern with method chaining for complex object construction.
pro.usecase: Real-world use: SQL query builders in ORM libraries.
